data.title: US FAA to review Boeing cockpit alerting system for MAX 10
data.description:
By David Shepardson

The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration said on Friday it will review Boeing's proposed enhanced flight crew alerting system for the Boeing 737 MAX 10, which will include a synthetic enhanced angle-of-attack system and a means to shut off stall warning and overspeed alerts.

In December 2022, Congress agreed to waive a deadline imposing a new safety standard for modern cockpit alerts for the MAX 7 and MAX 10, but required retrofitting planes with future safety enhancements within three years of the MAX 10 certification. The requirements were adopted by Congress as part of aircraft certification reform passed after two fatal 737 MAX crashes killed 346 people in Indonesia and Ethiopia and led to the plane's 20-month grounding.

The FAA said it will also review and certify design changes that will incorporate the required safety enhancements on all other models of Boeing 737 MAX aircraft and monitor Boeing to ensure the company takes actions necessary to provide service information to airlines in time to meet required deadlines.

Boeing declined to immediately comment.

Boeing, which currently offers the MAX 8 and MAX 9 for sale, has struggled for years to win FAA approval for the new versions of the MAX - the shorter MAX 7 and longer 10. On Thursday, Southwest Airlines CEO Bob Jordan told Reuters he expects the MAX 7 to be certified by August 2026, with the U.S. carrier to start flying the small single-aisle jet during the first quarter of 2027.

Boeing has faced delays in the certification of its MAX 7 and MAX 10 models due to an engine de-icing issue. The U.S. planemaker has also faced delays in certifying its widebody 777X jet. In October, the FAA gave Boeing approval to raise its 737 MAX production to 42 planes per month, ending a 38-plane cap in place since January 2024. The FAA imposed the unprecedented production cap shortly after a 2024 mid-air emergency involving a new Alaska Airlines 737 MAX 9 that was missing four key bolts in a door plug, causing a gaping hole to open in the fuselage at 16,000 feet (4,900 m). The incident revealed widespread production safety and quality lapses at Boeing.

data.title: 2 MSEK for Research to Improve Horse Welfare and Diagnostics
data.description:
Agria Petinsure

Six new research projects on horses have been awarded funding by the Foundation for Equine Research, with 2 MSEK coming from the Agria Research Fund. This year’s topics focus, among other things, on improving horse welfare in dressage, reducing the culture of silence within equestrian sports, as well as developing improved methods for diagnosing common diseases and injuries.

“The topics of this year’s selected projects are important both for the well-being of horses and for the future of equestrian sports,” says David Haak, CEO of Agria.

A new assessment protocol aims to shift the focus at dressage competitions. Instead of identifying shortcomings, the project seeks to help the sport recognize and reward harmony in line with good horse welfare. In close collaboration with the equestrian federation, active participants in the sport, and researchers, an assessment protocol will be developed with horse welfare and harmony at its core.

Another important question to be investigated is: Why is it difficult to speak up when horse welfare is compromised? By examining social and psychological barriers, researchers hope to reduce the culture of silence and thereby contribute to greater community within the equestrian world and increase the legitimacy of equestrian sports in the eyes of the public.

One of the veterinary projects will investigate improved diagnostics with a focus on horses with EMS (Equine Metabolic Syndrome), which is associated with high insulin levels. The new diagnostic method is based on horse-specific analyses of C-peptide and is expected to provide a more reliable picture of whether elevated insulin levels are due to increased production or reduced ability to metabolize insulin in the individual horse.

Another project focuses on improving the diagnosis of lameness by examining the effects of lunging, flexion tests, and local anesthesia on the degree of lameness. Objective analysis methods have revealed shortcomings in traditional lameness examinations that can lead to incorrect diagnoses, and now the diagnostic methods will be further investigated.

Additional projects aim to develop an analysis method for detecting tapeworms in horses quickly and cost-effectively, in order to prevent colic and other health problems.

Moreover, researchers want to focus on understanding which gene variants increase or decrease the risk of infectious diseases. The results may in the future be used both in breeding and for more effective treatments.

“The foundation has high hopes for this year’s projects. The approved projects related to horse welfare address current issues and have an exemplary collaborative approach between research and sport. In addition, other approved projects are expected to improve horse health and welfare in several ways through better diagnostics and more precise knowledge of the individual horse’s conditions and disease profiles, which will provide important tools for the future,” says Lina Bengtsson, Research Secretary, the Foundation for Equine Research.

New Projects Awarded Funding for 2026

  • Defining and Grading Harmony: Towards a Complementary Assessment Protocol in Line with Good Horse Welfare at Dressage CompetitionsElke Hartmann, Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences (SLU)
  • Speaking Up for Animal Welfare in Equestrian SportsHans Erik Næss, Kristiania University CollegeSusanne Johansson, Swedish School of Sport and Health Sciences (GIH)
  • Why Are Some Horses More Prone to Infections While Others in the Group Develop Immunity?Thomas Bergström, SLU
  • The Importance of Confounding Factors in the Evaluation of Lameness in HorsesEmma Persson-Sjödin, SLU
  • Improved Diagnosis of Tapeworms Using LAMP PCRCarl Peter Halvarsson, SLU
  • Use of Horse-Specific Insulin and C-Peptide Tests to Improve Diagnosis and Monitoring of Insulin DysregulationEmma Margareta Strage, SLUHege Brun-Hansen, Norwegian University of Life Sciences

For more information, please contact:

Damien Hanley

Marketing Strategy Manager

press@agriapetinsure.ie

Swedish owned Agria, the world's oldest specialist pet insurer, has been helping pets through illness and injury since 1890 and operates in Ireland as Agria Petinsure. With a reputation for reliability and customer-focused services, Agria Petinsure continues to innovate and deliver insurance solutions that meet the evolving needs of pets and pet owners.

data.title: Small Changes to European AT1 Bonds Possible, Could Cause Spreads to Widen — Market Talk
data.description: European additional tier one bonds, or AT1 bonds, could undergo small changes after the European Central Bank proposed modifications to the bonds, ING's Suvi Kosonen says in a note. AT1 bonds are lower-ranked bonds issued by banks which act as capital buffers. While one proposal to abolish AT1 bonds is less likely to be implemented, small changes to the debt instruments are possible, she says. "Smaller changes could eventually end up being negative for AT1 spreads that are currently at very tight levels in a longer historical comparison." (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Fitch Affirms Entel at 'BBB-', Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Chicago-12 December 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Empresa Nacional de Telecomunicaciones S.A.'s (Entel) Long-Term Foreign Currency (FC) and Local Currency (LC) Issuer Default Rating (IDR) and senior unsecured bonds at 'BBB-'. Fitch has also affirmed the National Long-Term Ratings and national senior issuances of Entel at 'AA-(cl)' and the equity rating of 'Primera Clase Nivel 1 (cl)'. The Rating Outlook is Stable.

The affirmation reflects Entel's robust business model, leading positions in Chile and Peru, strong brand recognition and high network quality, which support company's ability to sustain its strong performance. The ratings also capture Entel's broadening revenue base in Chile and Peru. Entel's ratings factor in the fierce competition, which pressures operational performance and deleveraging capacity.

The Stable Outlook considers Fitch's expectation that gross and net leverage of 3.5x and 3.0x, respectively, will remain under our rating sensitivities for an investment-grade rating despite a higher investment plan in the following years and a strong competitive environment.

Key Rating Drivers

Steady Leverage Evolution Expected: Fitch expects gross and net debt/EBITDA to remain below 3.0x and 2.5x, respectively, over the rating horizon, supported by gradual EBITDA growth despite high capex and dividends. Gross and net leverage were 2.6x and 2.3x, respectively, in LTM September 2025, in line with prior forecasts, and lower than the 2.8x and 2.3x at YE 2024. The completed refinancing extended maturities and reduced financing costs.

Competition Weighs on EBITDA Margin: Fitch forecasts EBITDA margins at 22%-23% in the medium term, pressured by competition in Chile and Peru, limited average revenue per user (ARPU) growth, and cost inflation. Expansion of the Fixed Home segment in Chile will weigh on margins until it reaches profitable scale. In 9M25, revenues rose 8.7% (Chile 6.6%; Peru 12.9%) on postpaid gains, and higher revenues from equipment, business-to-business (B2B), and Fixed Home segments. EBITDA margin fell to 22.1% in 9M25 from 22.7% at YE 2024.

Interest in TMCH's Assets: Fitch's base case excludes large M&A, However, Entel has shown interest in Telefónica Móviles Chile S.A (TMCH; BB-/Negative) assets. Given Entel's mobile leadership in mobile business, Fitch believes the target would likely be TMCH's fixed internet operations, serving 1.3 million users with CLP264 billion in 2024 revenue. A potential deal could enhance service diversification and support Entel's 20%-25% market share goal for 2028. Any fiber consolidation between TMCH and Entel would likely need regulatory approval, as combined share could reach 50%. Fitch will monitor the potential transaction, timing, and impact on credit metrics.

Leading Mobile Brand in Chile: The ratings consider Entel's strong brand and market share in the mobile segment in Chile. The company is the leading provider of mobile voice and broadband services, with a customer base of 10.1 million as of 3Q25 and a market share of around 35% in terms of subscribers. The company is well-positioned in terms of spectrum and network development to benefit from 5G adoption in the long term.

Scale and Profitability Focus in Peru: Fitch expects Peru to reach an EBITDA margin of about 26% in 2026. In 9M25, the margin was 25% (18% including leases), similar to 2024. Entel aims to gain three to five percentage points in revenue share and reach 28%-30% in EBITDA margin by 2028. The operation is expanding mobile reach, with 10.4 million subscribers as of September 2025 (9.9 million in September 2024), about 22% market share, 24% revenue share, and contributing 35% of revenues and 32% of EBITDA.

Negative FCF Expected: Fitch expects Entel to post negative FCF in the medium term as capex and dividends (80% of net income) consume CFO. Capex focuses on 5G rollout in Chile and Peru and fiber-to-the-home (FTTH) deployment. Fitch forecasts capex/revenue at about 20% over the next two years, easing to 18.5% in 2028. This profile would keep FCF negative at about CLP150 billion in 2026-2027. This is offset by the parent's flexibility in dividend distribution to sustain leverage in line with investment-grade levels.

Equalization with Almendral Ratings: Fitch equalizes the ratings of Entel and its holding company Almendral S.A. (Almendral; AA-[cl]/Stable) under its parent-subsidiary criteria. Equalization reflects Entel's dominant contribution to Almendral's consolidated figures, the immaterial level of holding debt in the consolidated profile (0.1%), the absence of ring-fencing and Almendral's control over Entel's dividend policy of Entel (55% of the ownership).

Equity Rating: Entel's equity rating is based on its strong credit profile, long track record in the stock market, 100% market presence, and 45.1% free float. Entel's market capitalization is about USD1.4 billion, and it is an important player in the Santiago stock market, with high daily trading volumes that have averaged USD2.0 million over the last month (as of December 2025).

Peer Analysis

Entel has a higher EBITDA margin and more conservative leverage profile than TMCH. Despite TMCH's more diversified cash flow generation in terms of types of services (fixed, mobile), Entel benefits from growing geographic diversification due to its operation in the Peruvian market and increased participation in the fiber business.

Millicom International Cellullar S.A (BB+/ Stable) has stronger profitability but a somewhat weaker leverage profile than Entel. Entel has lower geographical and service diversification than Millicom. Millicom's ratings are constrained by the operating environments of its operating subsidiaries, which contribute significant upstream cash flows.

UNE EPM Telecomunicaciones S.A. (Tigo UNE; BB+/Stable) has a stronger financial profile than Entel, which has net leverage below 2.0x and similar EBITDA margins in the mid-20% range. Despite this, the lower rating of Tigo UNE compared to Entel is limited by its Millicom current rating, the owner of Tigo UNE, and lower geographic diversification compared with Entel. Tigo UNE has relatively more balanced cash flow diversification between mobile and fixed services.

Colombia Telecomunicaciones SA ESP (ColTel; BB+/Stable) has lower profitability and higher net leverage than Entel. The company benefits from its position as the leading mobile player in Chile, while ColTel has a weaker market position. Tigo UNE and ColTel are concentrated in Colombia, which is a riskier market than Chile.

Fitch’s Key Rating-Case Assumptions

-- An 8.7% of increase in revenues in 2025, and a mid-single-digit revenue increase during the 2026-2028 period. The EBITDA margin is expected to remain around 22%-23% in the medium term (after lease adjustments) for the following periods;

-- EBITDA margin in Peru of about 26% in 2025 (19% after lease adjustments), increasing to 27% in 2027, with customer base increase to 10.7 million in 2027;

-- Dividend distribution of 80% of net income for the 2024-2027 period;

-- Low double-digit growth of revenues of fixed business due to an increase in fiber customers under the Onnet contract;

-- Capital intensity of around 18.5% in 2025, 20% in 2026 and 2027, and returning to 18% in 2028. Around 60% of this capex is accounted for in Chile and remaining 40% in Peru, based on 5G and fiber deployment in both countries.

RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

-- Total debt to EBITDA or net debt to EBITDA consistently above 3.5x and 3.0x, respectively, due to weakness in Peru, sustained deterioration in Chile, or higher-than-forecast shareholder distributions or higher-than-projected investment requirements;

-- Failure to maintain the EBITDA margin above 20% sustainably in the medium term;

-- Inability to sustain a positive trend in the CFO minus capex to debt ratio, moving toward neutral to positive territory in the medium term.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

-- Stronger-than-expected FCF generation in Peru due to a combination of EBITDA improvement and reduced investment requirements, while maintaining the company's cash flow generation in Chile and measured shareholder returns;

-- (CFO-capex)/debt consistently over 10% in the medium term;

-- Total debt to EBITDA and net debt to EBITDA sustained below 2.5x and 2.0x, respectively.

Liquidity and Debt Structure

Entel's liquidity is strong. As of September 2025, cash and deposits were CLP239 billion, equal to 2.0x short-term debt of CLP119 billion. Short-term debt mainly comprises a CLP32 billion bank loan in Peru and CLP75 billion of reverse factoring and confirming. The next relevant maturity is a local bond of CLP247 billion due in 2028.

Financial flexibility improved after the 2025 refinancing program. Actions included payment of the 2025 international bond amortization of CLP281 billion, early repayment of the 2026 international bond amortization of CLP281 billion, and repayment of an international bank loan of CLP38 billion. In 2025, Entel raised CLP254 billion of new debt via a local bond and bank loans, and received CLP202 billion from CCS contract restructuring. Entel has strong market access, with undrawn committed credit lines of around CLP100 billion and available national bond programs of UF10 million.

Issuer Profile

Entel is a leading Chilean telecommunications company that provides mobile and fixed-line services to consumers and businesses in Chile and Peru.

Summary of Financial Adjustments

-- Lease adjustments;

-- Hedge derivatives on financial debt;

-- Factoring and confirming adjustments

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. Public Ratings with Credit Linkage to other ratings

The ratings of Entel are linked to Almendral S.A Ratings due to the "Parent Subsidiary Linkage Criteria."

MACROECONOMIC ASSUMPTIONS AND SECTOR FORECASTS Click here to access Fitch's latest quarterly Global Corporates Sector Forecasts Monitor data file which aggregates key data points used in our credit analysis. Fitch's macroeconomic forecasts, commodity price assumptions, default rate forecasts, sector key performance indicators and sector-level forecasts are among the data items included. The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq slip as Broadcom adds to AI bubble angst
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes: Dow up 0.31%, S&P 500 slips 0.18%, Nasdaq off 0.51%
  • Broadcom falls as margin pressures add to AI payoff jitters
  • Lululemon climbs after CEO exit, profit forecast boost

By Johann M Cherian and Pranav Kashyap

The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq slipped on Friday as Broadcom's latest results added to concerns about an AI-fueled bubble, dampening optimism stoked by the Federal Reserve's less-hawkish-than-expected signals on 2026 rate cuts.

Broadcom slid 8.4% after the chipmaker warned of slimmer future margins on its AI system sales, despite projecting strong quarterly revenue. This sharpened worries about the profitability of surging AI investments.

Other chip stocks, such as Advanced Micro Devices lost 1% and a broader chips index fell 1.5% a day after Oracle unveiled a weak forecast.

Shares of the cloud company fell 2.3% after logging their biggest daily drop since January in the previous session.

"Given the fact that 'Big Tech' has been at the vanguard of the rally since October 2022, there's a danger that it may become the catalyst for broad-based selling," said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

At 9:47 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 154.56 points, or 0.31%, to 48,856.46, the S&P 500 lost 12.58 points, or 0.18%, to 6,888.92 and the Nasdaq Composite lost 119.55 points, or 0.51%, to 23,478.83.

Nine of the 11 S&P 500 sectors edged higher, although heavyweight tech stocks lost 1%, pulling down the overall index.

Despite the gloomy outlook, the S&P 500 , the Dow and the Russell 2000 all closed at record highs on Thursday and were on track for weekly gains after the Fed trimmed borrowing costs and delivered a less hawkish outlook than investors had feared.

The biggest gains were in the blue-chip and small-cap indexes, underscoring a shift into value stocks away from the mega-cap names. The Russell 2000 has outpaced the S&P 500 for much of this quarter as investors rotate out of AI-fueled growth names and into value-heavy sectors such as healthcare .

"There's plenty of cash on the sidelines, the consumer is not as dire as many proclaimed, and the Fed is easing, not tightening. Plus, many sectors and stocks are well rested and ready to run," said Robert Edwards, chief investment officer, Edwards Asset Management.

Still, traders are pricing in a total of 50 basis points of rate cuts by the end of 2026 – more than the Fed signaled on Wednesday.

Among others, Nvidia edged up 0.3% after a report said the company is evaluating an increase in H200 chip production to meet robust demand from China.

Lululemon Athletica <LULU.O> jumped 12% after the apparel maker raised its annual profit forecast and said that CEO Calvin McDonald was leaving the company.

In other single-stock moves, U.S.-listed shares of cannabis companies rose after a report said President Donald Trump was looking to cut restrictions on marijuana through a planned order. Canopy Growth added 23%, and Tilray Brands climbed 30%.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.55-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and by a 1.1-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted 24 new 52-week highs and one new low, while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 66 new highs and 37 new lows.

data.title: Sustainable Fitch Affirms Akiş's Entity Rating at '2'
data.description:
Related Content: Akis Gayrimenkul Yatirim Ortakligi A.S. - Entity Rating

Sustainable Fitch-London-12 December 2025: Sustainable Fitch has affirmed Akiş Gayrimenkul Yatirim Ortakligi A.S.’s (Akiş) Entity Rating at '2' and increased its entity score to 73 from 70. This reflects its continued implementation of comprehensive sustainability measures, including the maintenance of BREEAM In-Use Excellent certifications for its two principal shopping centres, Akasya and Akbatı, which together comprise the majority of its portfolio.

Akiş is a REIT based in Türkiye, primarily focused on shopping centre investments. The company’s portfolio is dominated by Akasya and Akbatı, representing about 94% of its current value, with the remainder formed by the Erenköy Apartment. Akiş is part of Akkök Holding, a diversified group with operations across chemicals, real estate, energy and composites.

The company’s business activities are aligned with its sustainability strategy, with both Akasya and Akbatı shopping centres holding BREEAM In-Use Excellent certifications. Akasya also hosts KidZania Istanbul, an educational entertainment facility supporting youth development and social inclusion, which brings positive social impact.

The company discloses Scopes 1 and 2 emissions; comprehensive Scope 3 categories; and energy, water and waste metrics. Akiş has measurable environmental targets, including long-term carbon-neutrality goals and annual reduction targets for Scopes 1, 2 and 3 emissions. Its total emissions improved overall in the past three years.

Socially, Akiş upholds international human and labour rights standards, with zero fatalities and no severe injuries reported for employees and contractors over the past three years. The company maintains a gender-diverse workforce, though women comprise 80% of senior management, indicating an imbalance at the top level.

Governance practices are robust, with adherence to the Akkök Holding group code of business ethics and a zero-incident track record. The board-level governance committee oversees remuneration, and executive variable pay is linked to well-defined financial targets. Financial reporting is publicly available and audited, with no major issues raised.

data.title: This Building Stock Is Having Its Best Day In Over a Year. Why Shares Are Rising 15%. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Shares of Quanex Building Products headed for their largest same-day percentage increase in nearly 17 years after the small-cap manufacturing company swung to a profit in its fiscal fourth quarter.

The company, which makes components for windows and doors, logged a fourth-quarter profit of $19.6 million, or 43 cents a share. This compares to a loss of $13.9 million, or 30 cents, in the same period last year.

The turnaround drove shares 24% higher to $18.71 in premarket trading Friday, putting Quanex on pace for its biggest percentage increase since a 31% jump on March 23, 2009, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Coming into Friday, shares have plunged 38% this year. The benchmark S&P 500, by comparison, has gained over 17%.

The quarterly profit appeared to overshadow weak points in the print as well as prudent messaging from the company. Operating income swelled to $42.9 million from $2.8 million a year earlier. However, sales dipped to $489.8 million from $492.2 million last year.

In prepared remarks, CEO George Wilson pointed to the company's improving liquidity, noting that its "strong cash flow" allowed Quanex to repay $75 million in bank debt in fiscal 2025.

"We continue to be encouraged by the overall resilience of the business in the current environment," Wilson said. Still, he conceded that inflationary pressures, housing affordability issues, and other headwinds caused end markets "to decline meaningfully as compared to 2024."

The CEO had a word of caution heading into fiscal 2026: Wilson warned that revenue and earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization could be flat over the prior fiscal year.

"The first half of 2026 may be more challenged than the first half of 2025, which would imply a somewhat improved second half year-over-year," the chief executive said.

Consequently, Quanex declined to provide "official guidance," saying it plans to revisit its fiscal guidance when it reports first-quarter earnings.

data.title: General Dynamics gets 'neutral' rating as Citigroup starts coverage
data.description:
** Citigroup starts coverage on Gulfstream jet maker General Dynamics with "neutral" rating and a price target of $371

** New PT represents an upside of 8.6% from stock's last close

** Brokerage says all four GD segments have steadily increased their backlog with drastic upticks in marine and technologies in recent quarters, alongside strong year-over-year revenue growth

** Marine Systems is poised to be a significant beneficiary with investments supporting Columbia and Virginia class subs - Citigroup

** Says that GD's recent FAA certification of G800 aircraft, next-gen Gulfstream large-cabin, is a key catalyst

** Fourteen of 24 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, nine "hold" and one "sell"; their median PT is $381, according to data compiled by LSEG

** Including session's moves, shares up 29% YTD
